requirement.title: Design Doc Schema — Location and Filesystem-Determined Shape
requirement.description: The design-doc schema MUST define (a) where design docs live, (b) how their identity (spec-id, iteration) is derived from the path, and (c) which conditional sections apply based on filesystem state. Per the "no modes" decision in design.md §2, the schema is single-behavior — a filesystem check drives the expected conditional sections; there is no mode selector. The schema rules live in scripts/lib/sdd-utils.js as the exported DESIGN_DOC_RULES constant (see fr-sd-010).

acceptance_criteria.criterion: The schema MUST specify design doc location as docs/plans/<spec-id>/<YYYY-MM-DD>/design.md, with spec-id derived from directory name (kebab-case) and iteration date derived from date directory name. REQ-F003

The schema MUST specify that filesystem state determines which conditional sections the refiner expects: specs/<spec-id>/spec.xml exists → expect Context + Change Intent; does not exist → expect prose with problem/solution/requirements/scope. This is a single-behavior filesystem check, not a mode selector. REQ-F003, REQ-F005

Section heading detection MUST tolerate trailing suffix text on the heading line (e.g., "## Context (from spec v1)", "## Context — 2026-04-19") while rejecting prefix words like "## Contextual Factors". The regex form is anchored with a word boundary after the keyword and accepts any non-newline content to end-of-line (equivalent to /^#+\s+Context\b[^\n]*$/im for Context, same shape for Change Intent). The explicit pattern lives in DESIGN_DOC_RULES.section_regex. REQ-F003, REQ-F005

requirement.title: Design Doc Schema — Structure When No Prior Spec Exists
requirement.description: The schema MUST define the minimum content requirements for a design doc produced when no prior spec exists for the target spec-id.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: The schema MUST specify minimum content for this case as: problem description or motivation, proposed solution or architecture, identifiable requirements (in prose), and scope declaration (includes/excludes). No fixed section headings required — the refiner extracts from prose. REQ-F003
The schema MUST include a valid example showing a complete design doc with all minimum content elements identifiable. REQ-F003
The schema MUST include an invalid example: an empty or stub design doc (e.g., only "TODO: fill in later"), with error message: "design doc has no substantive content". REQ-F008

requirement.title: Design Doc Schema — Structure When Prior Spec Exists
requirement.description: The schema MUST define the required sections for a design doc produced when a prior spec exists for the target spec-id.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: The schema MUST specify two required sections: Context (current spec state summary, 2-3 sentences, spec version reference) and Change Intent (what is changing and why). Missing either section is ERROR. REQ-F005
The schema MUST specify one recommended section: Architecture Impact (how changes interact with existing design). Optional for simple changes, recommended for cross-cutting changes. REQ-F005
The schema MUST include a valid example with Context, Change Intent, and Architecture Impact sections. REQ-F005

The schema MUST include invalid examples: (1) missing Context section with error "iteration design doc missing required Context section", (2) missing Change Intent section with error "iteration design doc missing required Change Intent section". REQ-F005, REQ-F008

The schema MUST forbid pre-authored structured delta sections (no explicit ADDED/MODIFIED/REMOVED lists in the design doc). The LLM refiner derives the delta from narrative intent; the design doc carries only human-authored narrative. REQ-F005

requirement.title: Design Doc Schema — Validation Summary
requirement.description: The schema MUST include a validation summary table showing all checks, their type (deterministic vs LLM judgment), and applicability per path.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: The validation summary MUST list these deterministic checks: file existence (ERROR if missing, always), substantive content (ERROR if empty/stub, always), Context section heading present (ERROR if missing, when prior spec exists), Change Intent section heading present (ERROR if missing, when prior spec exists), stale iteration date (WARNING if date <= spec's design_iteration, when prior spec exists). REQ-F003, REQ-F005, REQ-F026

The validation summary MUST list these LLM judgment checks: requirements identifiable in prose (ERROR if none found, when no prior spec exists), scope declared (ERROR if missing, when no prior spec exists). REQ-F003

requirement.title: Spec Identity Header Schema — Required Fields
requirement.description: scripts/lib/sdd-schemas/spec.md MUST define all required fields in the spec.xml identity header (<overview> element).

acceptance_criteria.criterion: The schema MUST define these always-required fields with types: spec_id (string, kebab-case, must match folder name), spec_version (positive integer, starts at 1), status (enum: "active"), title (string, human-readable), description (string, brief scope summary), source/design_path (string, valid file path to design doc), source/design_iteration (date, YYYY-MM-DD). Missing any field is ERROR. REQ-F010
The schema MUST define the conditionally-required supersedes field: required when spec_version > 1, format "<spec-id>:v<N>". Missing supersedes for v2+ is ERROR. REQ-F010

The schema MUST define the scope element: includes (list of features with id attribute and text description, always required) and excludes (list of reasons, recommended). Empty includes is WARNING. REQ-F010

requirement.title: Spec Identity Header Schema — Detail File Structure
requirement.description: The schema MUST define the structure of detail files referenced from spec.xml.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: The schema MUST specify that spec.xml references detail files via <detail_file path="details/<name>.xml" /> elements, and each detail file contains <requirement> elements. REQ-F012

The schema MUST specify each <requirement> element requires: id attribute (unique, e.g., "fr-bs-001"), <title>, <description>, and <acceptance_criteria> with at least one <criterion>. Each criterion MUST have an id attribute and a <trace> element referencing the source requirement. REQ-F012

requirement.title: Spec Identity Header Schema — Examples
requirement.description: The schema MUST include valid and invalid examples for LLM pattern-matching.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: The schema MUST include a valid v1 spec example: complete <overview> with all required fields, no supersedes, scope with includes and excludes. REQ-F010
The schema MUST include a valid v2+ spec example: complete <overview> with supersedes set to "<spec-id>:v1", updated source pointing to new design iteration. REQ-F010

The schema MUST include invalid examples with error messages: (1) missing spec_version, (2) broken design_path pointing to non-existent file, (3) missing supersedes for v2+, (4) empty scope as WARNING. REQ-F010, REQ-F008

requirement.title: SDD Utils — Design Doc Validator API
requirement.description: scripts/lib/sdd-utils.js MUST export functions for parsing and validating design docs. These implement the deterministic checks from fr-sd-004.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: MUST export parseDesignDoc(filePath: string) that returns {spec_id, iteration, hasPriorSpec, hasContext, hasChangeIntent, hasSubstantiveContent, specDesignIteration} derived from the file path and content. Returns null if file not found. spec_id and iteration are extracted from the directory path, not from file content. hasPriorSpec is a plain filesystem fact (does specs/<spec_id>/spec.xml exist?) — it is NOT a "mode" and the return type MUST NOT expose a "mode" field, per design.md §2 and fr-bs-002. REQ-F003, REQ-F005
MUST export validateDesignDoc(parsed) that returns {valid: boolean, issues: Array<{level: "ERROR"|"WARNING"|"INFO", field: string, message: string}>}. valid is true when zero ERRORs. REQ-F008

validateDesignDoc deterministic checks: file exists (ERROR), content not empty/stub (ERROR), when prior spec exists: Context heading present (ERROR), Change Intent heading present (ERROR), iteration date not stale vs spec's design_iteration (WARNING). REQ-F003, REQ-F005, REQ-F026

requirement.title: SDD Utils — Spec Header Validator API
requirement.description: scripts/lib/sdd-utils.js MUST export functions for parsing and validating spec.xml identity headers.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: MUST export parseSpecHeader(specXmlContent: string) that returns {spec_id, spec_version, status, design_path, design_iteration, scope, supersedes?} or null if header not found. Parses the <overview> element from XML content. REQ-F010
MUST export validateSpecHeader(parsed) that returns {valid: boolean, issues: Array<{level, field, message}>}. valid is true when zero ERRORs. REQ-F010

validateSpecHeader deterministic checks: all required fields present (ERROR per missing field), spec_version is positive integer (ERROR), design_path points to existing file (ERROR), design_iteration matches YYYY-MM-DD format (ERROR), supersedes present when spec_version > 1 (ERROR), supersedes format matches "<spec-id>:v<N>" (ERROR), empty scope includes (WARNING). REQ-F010

requirement.title: Schema Rules Are Exported Constants (Single SoT)
requirement.description:
Design-doc and spec-header schema rules MUST live as exported constants in scripts/lib/sdd-utils.js (DESIGN_DOC_RULES and SPEC_HEADER_RULES). Validators (parseDesignDoc, validateDesignDoc, parseSpecHeader, validateSpecHeader) MUST consume these constants — not duplicate the rules as regex literals inside the function bodies. Downstream consumers (skills, tests, the print-schema CLI) MUST read from these exports rather than redescribing the schema in hand-authored text.
This is the structural defense against the drift that produced the 2026-04-19 "Context heading" bug: the skill said one thing, the schema doc said a second, and the validator regex enforced a third. With rules centralized, validators and documentation are guaranteed to agree by construction.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: scripts/lib/sdd-utils.js MUST export a frozen DESIGN_DOC_RULES object containing: canonical_path, path_regex, substantive_min_chars, section_regex (with Context and ChangeIntent entries), iteration.required_sections, iteration.recommended_sections, iteration.forbidden_section_keywords, initial.required_prose_elements. REQ-F003, REQ-F005

scripts/lib/sdd-utils.js MUST export a frozen SPEC_HEADER_RULES object containing: canonical_path, design_iteration_regex, supersedes_regex, required_fields, conditional_fields, scope, delta (with placement, ordering, last_delta_invariants, and child_element_rules). REQ-F010

Validator functions (parseDesignDoc, validateDesignDoc, parseSpecHeader, validateSpecHeader) MUST reference the exported rule constants for regexes and thresholds. Duplicating a rule as a local regex literal inside a function body is ERROR and constitutes drift risk. REQ-F003, REQ-F005, REQ-F010

requirement.title: Schema Access Pattern — print-schema CLI
requirement.description:
Skills and agents that need to know an SDD artifact's schema MUST obtain it by invoking scripts/lib/print-schema.js, not by reading hand-authored markdown or by copying templates into SKILL.md bodies. The CLI reads directly from the exported rule constants (fr-sd-010), so there is exactly one source of truth and the output cannot drift from the validator's behavior.
This requirement exists because hand-authored schema copies in skills produced the 2026-04-19 "Context heading" bug. Skills MUST NOT embed structural templates; they link to the CLI instead.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: scripts/lib/print-schema.js MUST exist and accept two targets (design, spec) plus a --json flag. Default output is human-readable text. --json emits the raw rule object with RegExp values encoded as <regex>pattern</regex> strings. REQ-F003, REQ-F010
print-schema.js MUST derive its output from the exported DESIGN_DOC_RULES and SPEC_HEADER_RULES constants in sdd-utils.js. It MUST NOT hand-author schema content. REQ-F003, REQ-F010

arc-brainstorming, arc-refining, and any other SDD-pipeline skill MUST reference print-schema.js (e.g., "run `node scripts/lib/print-schema.js design`") as the canonical way to get the schema. SDD skills MUST NOT embed structural code-block templates that duplicate schema content; REQUIRED BACKGROUND links to print-schema.js instead. Skills also MUST NOT reference hand-authored markdown in scripts/lib/sdd-schemas/ as schema sources of truth (those files are deprecated pointers to the CLI and constants). REQ-F003, REQ-F005, REQ-F010
